title: Idli
description: A practical home-style Idli recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse and soak the rice and urad dal separately for 4 to 6 hours.
Grind the dal with small additions of water for 4 to 5 minutes until very fluffy. Grind the rice to a fine, slightly grainy batter.
Mix both batters with salt for 2 minutes. Cover loosely and ferment for 8 to 12 hours in a warm place until airy and increased in volume.
Gently fold the batter without beating out the air. Grease the idli moulds and fill each cup three-quarters full.
Bring 2 cups water to a steady boil in a steamer. Steam the idli over medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes without opening the lid.
Rest for 2 minutes, loosen with a wet spoon and serve hot with sambar and chutney.

title: Medu Vada
description: A practical home-style Medu Vada recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Soak urad dal for 4 hours, drain very well and grind with only 2 to 4 tablespoons cold water for 4 to 5 minutes until thick and fluffy.
Beat the batter by hand for 2 minutes to incorporate air. Fold in salt, chilli, ginger, curry leaves and any listed spices.
Heat oil in a deep pan to 175°C (350°F). Keep a bowl of water nearby for shaping.
Wet one hand, place 2 tablespoons batter on the palm, make a hole in the centre and slide carefully into the oil.
Fry in batches for 4 to 5 minutes, turning several times, until evenly golden and crisp.
Drain on a rack and serve immediately with sambar and chutney.

title: Uttapam
description: A practical home-style Uttapam recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Use well-fermented dosa batter at room temperature. Stir in enough water to make a thick, pourable batter and add salt.
Prepare the chopped onion, tomato, chilli, coriander and other toppings and keep them dry.
Heat a cast-iron griddle over medium heat to about 190°C (375°F) and grease it lightly.
Pour 1/2 cup batter and spread gently into a thick 15 cm circle; do not spread it thin like dosa.
Scatter 1/3 cup toppings over the wet surface and press lightly. Drizzle 1 teaspoon oil around the edges.
Cook for 2 to 3 minutes, flip and cook the topped side for 1 to 2 minutes until browned and the centre is set.
Serve immediately with chutney and sambar.

title: Pongal
description: A practical home-style Pongal recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 4 to 5 minutes until fragrant but not dark.
Rinse the rice and roasted dal, then pressure-cook with the measured water and salt for 12 minutes, or simmer covered for 30 to 35 minutes, until very soft.
Mash lightly with a spoon; the consistency should be creamy and loose rather than dry.
Heat ghee over medium heat. Fry cashews for 1 minute until golden and remove.
In the same ghee, fry cumin, crushed pepper, ginger and curry leaves for 30 to 45 seconds.
Pour the tempering over the rice mixture and stir for 1 minute. Add hot water if needed to loosen.
Top with cashews and serve hot.

title: Upma
description: A practical home-style Upma recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Dry-roast the semolina over medium-low heat for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring continuously, until aromatic; transfer to a bowl.
Heat oil over medium heat. Add mustard seeds, urad dal and curry leaves and fry for 30 seconds.
Add onion and chilli and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until soft, then add any listed vegetables and sauté for 3 minutes.
Pour in the measured water, add salt and bring to a full boil.
Lower the heat and add semolina in a slow stream while whisking continuously for 1 to 2 minutes to prevent lumps.
Cover and cook on low for 3 minutes, then turn off the heat and rest for 2 minutes.
Fluff with a fork, add lemon and coriander and serve hot.

title: Appam
description: A practical home-style Appam recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Soak the raw rice for 4 hours, then drain. Blend it with cooked rice and coconut milk for 3 to 4 minutes until very smooth.
Stir in yeast, sugar and salt. The batter should pour in a thick ribbon; add water only as needed.
Cover loosely and ferment in a warm place for 6 to 8 hours until lightly bubbly and increased in volume.
Stir gently and thin with a little water to a pourable consistency without knocking out all the air.
Heat an appam pan over medium heat, lightly grease and pour in about 1/3 cup batter.
Lift and swirl the pan so a thin layer coats the sides while the centre remains thicker. Cover and cook for 2 to 3 minutes without flipping.
Remove when the lacy edges are crisp and the centre is soft and set.

title: Puttu
description: A practical home-style Puttu recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix salt into the rice flour. Sprinkle in warm water 1 tablespoon at a time while rubbing the flour between the fingers.
Continue until the mixture resembles damp crumbs that hold together when pressed but break apart easily. Rest for 10 minutes.
Bring water to a strong boil in the puttu steamer.
Layer 2 tablespoons grated coconut, a layer of moistened flour and more coconut in the cylinder without packing tightly.
Attach the cylinder and steam for 5 to 7 minutes after steady steam appears from the top.
Rest for 1 minute, push the cylinder out carefully and serve hot with kadala curry or banana.

title: Avial
description: A practical home-style Avial recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 5 cups mixed vegetables cut into batons
steps: Wash and dry the vegetables thoroughly, then cut them into even pieces so they cook at the same rate.
Heat oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add whole spices or aromatics and stir for 30 to 45 seconds until fragrant.
Add firm vegetables first and sauté for 4 to 6 minutes. Add quick-cooking leafy vegetables later so they retain colour and texture.
Add tomatoes, sauce ingredients and ground spices. Cook over medium heat for 5 to 8 minutes, stirring every minute.
Add only the measured water or stock, cover and cook until just tender. Avoid overcooking; most vegetables need 8 to 15 minutes.
Uncover and cook over high heat for 2 to 4 minutes to remove excess moisture and concentrate the sauce.
Finish with lemon, herbs, sesame oil, cream or garam masala as listed and serve immediately.

title: Sambar
description: A practical home-style Sambar recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse the dal and pressure-cook with turmeric and 3 cups water for 12 minutes, or simmer for 30 minutes, until very soft; mash smooth.
Cook the vegetables and tomato in 2 cups water over medium heat for 10 to 15 minutes until almost tender.
Add tamarind, sambar powder and salt and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the mashed dal and enough hot water for a pourable consistency. Simmer gently for 10 minutes, stirring twice.
Heat oil, crackle mustard seeds, then add curry leaves, dried chillies and asafoetida for 30 seconds.
Pour the tempering over the sambar, cover for 2 minutes and serve hot.

title: Rasam
description: A practical home-style Rasam recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Crush or blend cumin, black pepper and garlic coarsely.
Combine tomatoes, tamarind water, rasam powder, the crushed spices, salt and water in a pot.
Bring to a gentle boil and simmer uncovered for 12 to 15 minutes until the raw tamarind aroma disappears.
Add cooked dal water when listed and heat until the surface becomes foamy; do not boil hard after this stage.
Heat oil or ghee and fry mustard seeds, curry leaves and dried chilli for 30 seconds.
Pour the tempering into the rasam, add coriander and serve hot.

title: Kerala Stew
description: A practical home-style Kerala Stew recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 500 g mixed vegetables or chicken pieces
steps: Heat coconut oil over medium heat and fry cinnamon, cloves and cardamom for 30 seconds.
Add onion, ginger and green chillies and cook for 4 minutes without browning.
Add chicken or vegetables and sauté for 4 to 5 minutes. Pour in thin coconut milk and season with salt.
Cover and simmer gently until tender, about 15 minutes for vegetables or 20 to 25 minutes for chicken.
Reduce the heat to very low and add thick coconut milk. Heat for 3 minutes without boiling, or the coconut milk may split.
Finish with curry leaves and black pepper and serve hot.

title: Kadala Curry
description: A practical home-style Kadala Curry recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Soak black chickpeas overnight, drain and pressure-cook with fresh water and salt for 25 to 30 minutes until tender.
Dry-roast coconut, coriander, chilli and spices over medium-low heat until deep golden, then cool and blend with water to a smooth paste.
Heat oil and fry mustard seeds and curry leaves for 30 seconds. Add onion and cook for 7 to 8 minutes until golden.
Add tomato and cook for 5 minutes, then stir in the roasted coconut paste.
Add chickpeas and 1 1/2 cups cooking liquid. Simmer uncovered for 12 to 15 minutes until thick.
Adjust salt and serve hot with puttu or appam.

title: Bisi Bele Bath
description: A practical home-style Bisi Bele Bath recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse rice and toor dal, then pressure-cook together with turmeric and 5 cups water for 15 minutes until very soft.
Cook the chopped vegetables separately in just enough water for 10 to 12 minutes until tender.
Combine rice, dal, vegetables, tamarind and bisi bele bath powder in a heavy pot.
Add hot water as needed and simmer over low heat for 12 minutes, stirring every 2 minutes because the mixture thickens quickly.
Heat ghee and fry mustard seeds, curry leaves, cashews and dried chilli for 45 seconds.
Stir the tempering into the rice, adjust salt and serve hot with a spoon of ghee.

title: Curd Rice
description: A practical home-style Curd Rice recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Cook the rice with extra water until very soft, then mash lightly while warm.
Cool the rice to room temperature before adding yogurt so the yogurt does not split.
Mix in yogurt, milk and salt for 1 minute until creamy. Add more milk if the mixture is too thick.
Heat oil, crackle mustard seeds and fry urad dal, curry leaves, chilli and ginger for 30 to 45 seconds.
Pour the tempering over the rice and mix gently.
Chill for 30 minutes or serve at room temperature, garnished with coriander or pomegranate.

title: Dhokla
description: A practical home-style Dhokla recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (356°F) and grease the specified pan. For steamed versions, bring water to a steady boil in the steamer instead.
Measure the dry ingredients accurately and whisk them together for 30 seconds so the leavening is evenly distributed.
In a separate bowl, whisk the wet ingredients for 1 to 2 minutes until smooth. Add the dry mixture and fold only until no dry patches remain.
Add fruit, nuts, vegetables or cheese as listed. Add baking soda or fruit salt last and fold gently for 15 to 20 seconds.
Transfer immediately to the pan. Bake until set and golden, generally 25 to 40 minutes, or steam for 15 to 20 minutes as appropriate.
A skewer should come out clean from a baked item. Cool for 10 minutes before cutting, glazing or adding syrup.

title: Khandvi
description: A practical home-style Khandvi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk gram flour, yogurt, water, turmeric, ginger-chilli paste and salt for 2 minutes until completely smooth.
Cook in a non-stick pan over medium heat for 8 to 12 minutes, stirring continuously, until very thick and the batter leaves the sides.
Test a teaspoon on a greased plate; it should spread thinly and roll after 1 minute. If it sticks, cook another minute.
Immediately spread the hot batter in a paper-thin layer over greased trays using a flat spatula.
Cool for 5 minutes, cut into strips and roll each strip gently.
Heat oil and fry mustard, sesame and curry leaves for 30 seconds. Pour over the rolls and garnish with coconut and coriander.

title: Undhiyu
description: A practical home-style Undhiyu recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2 cups surti papdi or green beans
steps: Wash and dry the vegetables thoroughly, then cut them into even pieces so they cook at the same rate.
Heat oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add whole spices or aromatics and stir for 30 to 45 seconds until fragrant.
Add firm vegetables first and sauté for 4 to 6 minutes. Add quick-cooking leafy vegetables later so they retain colour and texture.
Add tomatoes, sauce ingredients and ground spices. Cook over medium heat for 5 to 8 minutes, stirring every minute.
Add only the measured water or stock, cover and cook until just tender. Avoid overcooking; most vegetables need 8 to 15 minutes.
Uncover and cook over high heat for 2 to 4 minutes to remove excess moisture and concentrate the sauce.
Finish with lemon, herbs, sesame oil, cream or garam masala as listed and serve immediately.

title: Thepla
description: A practical home-style Thepla recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine flour, yeast or leavening, sugar and salt. Add the wet ingredients and mix with a spoon until a rough dough forms.
Knead by hand for 8 to 10 minutes, or with a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook for 6 minutes on medium-low speed, until smooth and elastic.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for 60 to 90 minutes, until doubled. Unleavened doughs need only a 20 to 30 minute rest.
Divide evenly, shape and add any filling or topping. Rest the shaped dough for 10 to 20 minutes while heating the cooking surface.
For oven-baked naan or enriched breads, preheat to 230°C (445°F) and bake for 6 to 12 minutes until puffed and browned. For griddle breads, cook over medium-high heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Brush with butter or ghee immediately after cooking and keep covered with a clean cloth until serving.

title: Handvo
description: A practical home-style Handvo recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (356°F) and grease the specified pan. For steamed versions, bring water to a steady boil in the steamer instead.
Measure the dry ingredients accurately and whisk them together for 30 seconds so the leavening is evenly distributed.
In a separate bowl, whisk the wet ingredients for 1 to 2 minutes until smooth. Add the dry mixture and fold only until no dry patches remain.
Add fruit, nuts, vegetables or cheese as listed. Add baking soda or fruit salt last and fold gently for 15 to 20 seconds.
Transfer immediately to the pan. Bake until set and golden, generally 25 to 40 minutes, or steam for 15 to 20 minutes as appropriate.
A skewer should come out clean from a baked item. Cool for 10 minutes before cutting, glazing or adding syrup.

title: Fafda
description: A practical home-style Fafda recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix gram flour, baking soda, carom seeds, turmeric and salt. Rub in the oil for 2 minutes.
Add warm water gradually and knead for 5 minutes into a firm, smooth dough. Rest covered for 20 minutes.
Divide into small balls. On an oiled board, press and drag each ball forward with the heel of the palm to make a thin strip.
Heat oil to 170°C (340°F). Fry the strips in small batches for 2 to 3 minutes until crisp but still yellow-golden.
Drain on a rack and serve hot with chutney or fried chillies.

title: Sev Tameta Nu Shaak
description: A practical home-style Sev Tameta Nu Shaak recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat oil over medium heat and crackle cumin and mustard seeds for 30 seconds.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, chilli, coriander powder and salt. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until pulpy.
Add water and jaggery and simmer for 5 minutes to balance the sweet, sour and spicy flavours.
Turn off the heat and stir in half the sev.
Transfer to a serving bowl and add the remaining sev just before serving so it stays partly crisp.

title: Gujarati Kadhi
description: A practical home-style Gujarati Kadhi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk yogurt, gram flour, water, sugar, ginger, chilli, turmeric and salt for 2 minutes until completely smooth.
Pour into a heavy saucepan and bring to a gentle boil over medium heat, whisking continuously for 5 minutes.
Reduce to low heat and simmer for 18 to 20 minutes, stirring every 2 to 3 minutes, until lightly thickened and the raw gram-flour taste disappears.
Heat ghee in a small pan over medium heat. Add mustard and cumin seeds and let them crackle for 20 seconds.
Add curry leaves and dried chillies and fry for another 20 seconds, then pour the hot tempering into the kadhi.
Simmer for 2 minutes, taste for the traditional sweet-sour balance and serve hot.

title: Khichu
description: A practical home-style Khichu recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Sesame seeds and chilli oil for serving
steps: Bring water to a full boil with cumin, chilli paste, baking soda, salt and 1 tablespoon oil.
Reduce to low heat and add rice flour gradually while stirring vigorously with a wooden spoon.
Continue mixing for 2 to 3 minutes until no dry flour remains and a thick dough forms.
Cover and steam over very low heat for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring once halfway through.
Serve hot with oil, sesame seeds and chilli powder.

title: Patra
description: A practical home-style Patra recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk gram flour, tamarind, jaggery, spices, salt and enough water into a thick, spreadable paste.
Trim the thick veins from the colocasia leaves. Place one leaf shiny-side down and spread a thin layer of paste over it.
Stack 3 to 4 leaves, fold in the sides and roll tightly from the stem end.
Steam the rolls over boiling water for 20 to 25 minutes, then cool for 15 minutes before slicing.
Heat oil, crackle mustard and sesame seeds, then sauté the slices for 4 to 5 minutes until lightly crisp.
Serve warm with coriander and coconut.

title: Dal Baati
description: A practical home-style Dal Baati recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse the mixed dals and pressure-cook with turmeric and water for 15 minutes until soft.
Prepare a tempering with ghee, cumin, garlic, chilli and tomato, cook for 5 minutes and stir it into the dal. Simmer for 8 minutes.
For baati, mix whole-wheat flour, semolina, salt and ghee. Add water gradually and knead for 4 minutes into a stiff dough.
Shape into firm balls and bake at 200°C (400°F) for 25 to 30 minutes, turning once, until browned and cooked through.
Crack the hot baati slightly and dip or brush generously with melted ghee.
Serve with the hot dal.

title: Churma
description: A practical home-style Churma recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix whole-wheat flour, semolina and half the ghee. Rub for 2 minutes, then add warm milk or water to form a stiff dough.
Shape into small thick discs and bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25 to 30 minutes, or deep-fry over medium-low heat until cooked through.
Cool completely, break into pieces and grind to coarse crumbs.
Mix in powdered sugar or jaggery, cardamom, nuts and the remaining warm ghee for 2 minutes.
Serve loose or press into small balls.

title: Gatte Ki Sabzi
description: A practical home-style Gatte Ki Sabzi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Knead gram flour, the first yogurt portion, oil, chilli, turmeric, carom and salt for 3 to 4 minutes into a firm dough.
Shape into 2 cm-thick logs and boil in salted water for 10 to 12 minutes until firm. Cool and slice, reserving 1 cup cooking water.
Whisk the remaining yogurt with coriander powder, turmeric and chilli until smooth.
Heat oil and cumin over medium heat. Lower the heat and add the yogurt mixture gradually while stirring continuously.
Cook for 6 to 8 minutes, add sliced gatte and reserved water, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Adjust salt and serve hot.

title: Ker Sangri
description: A practical home-style Ker Sangri recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse ker and sangri thoroughly, soak overnight and boil in fresh water for 15 minutes; drain and rinse again.
Heat oil over medium heat and fry cumin, fennel and asafoetida for 30 seconds.
Add the boiled ker sangri, turmeric, chilli, coriander powder and salt and sauté for 5 minutes.
Add yogurt or a small splash of water, cover and cook on low for 10 minutes.
Finish with amchur and garam masala and cook uncovered for 2 minutes.

title: Panchmel Dal
description: A practical home-style Panchmel Dal recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1/4 cup each moong, masoor, chana, toor and urad dal
steps: Rinse the lentils, beans or chickpeas thoroughly. Soak only when listed, then drain.

Place them in a pot with the measured water and turmeric. Bring to a boil, skim any foam, then simmer gently until tender—20 to 30 minutes for split lentils or 60 to 90 minutes for soaked whole beans.
In a separate pan, heat oil or ghee over medium heat. Cook onion for 7 to 9 minutes until golden, then add ginger-garlic paste for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes and ground spices and cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring often, until the masala thickens and the oil separates.
Add the cooked pulses with enough cooking liquid to reach the desired consistency. Simmer uncovered for 10 to 15 minutes.
Mash about one-quarter of the pulses against the side of the pot to thicken naturally. Adjust salt and acidity.
Prepare any final tempering in hot ghee for 30 to 45 seconds and pour it over the dish just before serving.

title: Laal Maas
description: A practical home-style Laal Maas recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 8 dried Mathania or mild red chillies, soaked
steps: Soak the dried red chillies in hot water for 20 minutes, then blend to a smooth paste.
Heat ghee in a heavy pot and fry whole spices and onions over medium heat for 10 minutes until deep golden.
Add the mutton and sear over high heat for 8 to 10 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste, chilli paste, yogurt and salt gradually, stirring continuously for 4 minutes.
Add 2 cups hot water, cover and simmer on low for 50 to 65 minutes until the meat is tender.
Uncover and reduce for 8 to 10 minutes until the gravy is glossy and the oil separates.

title: Safed Maas
description: A practical home-style Safed Maas recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Blend yogurt, cashews or almonds, poppy seeds and cream into a smooth paste.
Heat ghee and fry whole spices for 30 seconds. Add onion paste and cook over low heat for 6 minutes without browning.
Add mutton and sear over medium-high heat for 8 minutes.
Lower the heat and add the yogurt-nut paste gradually, stirring continuously for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add hot water, cover and simmer for 50 to 60 minutes until tender.
Finish with white pepper and cardamom and simmer uncovered for 5 minutes.

title: Bajra Khichdi
description: A practical home-style Bajra Khichdi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Drain the soaked millet and pulse it briefly in a processor to crack the grains.
Rinse with moong dal and place in a pressure cooker with water, turmeric and salt.
Pressure-cook for 18 to 20 minutes until very soft, or simmer covered for about 60 minutes.
Mash lightly and add hot water if needed for a soft porridge consistency.
Heat ghee, fry cumin for 30 seconds and stir the tempering into the khichdi.
Serve hot with yogurt or extra ghee.

title: Papad Ki Sabzi
description: A practical home-style Papad Ki Sabzi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Roast the papads over a flame or dry griddle until crisp, then break into large pieces.
Whisk yogurt, gram flour, turmeric, chilli and coriander powder with 1 cup water until smooth.
Heat oil and cumin over medium heat. Lower the heat and pour in the yogurt mixture while stirring continuously.
Add the remaining water and simmer gently for 8 to 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add papad pieces and cook for only 2 minutes so they soften without dissolving.
Adjust salt carefully because papad is already salty.

title: Moong Dal Pakodi
description: A practical home-style Moong Dal Pakodi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups split yellow moong dal, soaked 3 hours
steps: Drain the soaked moong dal thoroughly and grind with only 2 to 3 tablespoons water into a coarse, thick batter.
Beat the batter by hand for 2 minutes, then fold in onion, chilli, cumin, coriander and salt.
Heat oil to 175°C (350°F).
Drop tablespoon portions into the oil and fry in small batches for 4 to 5 minutes, turning until crisp and golden.
Drain on a rack and serve immediately with chutney.

title: Chole Bhature
description: A practical home-style Chole Bhature recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Cook soaked chickpeas until tender, reserving 2 cups cooking liquid.
Heat oil and cook onion for 8 minutes. Add ginger-garlic, tomatoes and chole masala and cook for 8 to 10 minutes until thick.
Add chickpeas and reserved liquid and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, mashing a few chickpeas to thicken. Finish with amchur.
For bhature, mix flour, yogurt, sugar, salt, baking soda and oil and knead for 7 minutes into a soft dough. Cover and rest for 1 hour.
Divide and roll into 15 cm ovals. Heat oil to 185°C (365°F).
Fry one at a time, pressing lightly with a slotted spoon until puffed, then turn and cook until pale golden.
Serve immediately with the hot chole.

title: Rajma Chawal
description: A practical home-style Rajma Chawal recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Soak kidney beans overnight, drain and pressure-cook with fresh water for 30 to 35 minutes until completely tender.
Heat oil over medium heat and cook onion for 8 minutes until golden. Add ginger-garlic for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes and spices and cook for 8 to 10 minutes until the masala thickens.
Add beans with 2 cups cooking liquid and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring every 4 minutes.
Mash a few beans to thicken, adjust salt and finish with garam masala.
Serve with freshly cooked rice.

title: Palak Paneer
description: A practical home-style Palak Paneer recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Wash and dry the vegetables thoroughly, then cut them into even pieces so they cook at the same rate.
Heat oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add whole spices or aromatics and stir for 30 to 45 seconds until fragrant.
Add firm vegetables first and sauté for 4 to 6 minutes. Add quick-cooking leafy vegetables later so they retain colour and texture.
Add tomatoes, sauce ingredients and ground spices. Cook over medium heat for 5 to 8 minutes, stirring every minute.
Add only the measured water or stock, cover and cook until just tender. Avoid overcooking; most vegetables need 8 to 15 minutes.
Uncover and cook over high heat for 2 to 4 minutes to remove excess moisture and concentrate the sauce.
Finish with lemon, herbs, sesame oil, cream or garam masala as listed and serve immediately.

title: Shahi Paneer
description: A practical home-style Shahi Paneer recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Blend the soaked cashews with 1/2 cup water until completely smooth.
Heat butter or ghee over medium-low heat. Cook onion, ginger and garlic for 6 minutes without deep browning.
Add tomatoes and spices and cook for 8 minutes, then blend the masala smooth and return it to the pan.
Whisk in the cashew paste and simmer for 4 minutes, stirring continuously.
Add paneer cubes and cream and cook on low for 5 to 6 minutes without boiling hard.
Finish with garam masala and dried fenugreek and serve hot.

title: Paneer Butter Masala
description: A practical home-style Paneer Butter Masala recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat half the butter and cook onion, ginger, garlic and tomatoes over medium heat for 10 minutes until very soft.
Cool slightly, blend until smooth and strain for a restaurant-style sauce.
Heat the remaining butter, add chilli, coriander and garam masala and stir for 20 seconds.
Add the blended sauce and simmer for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring often.
Add paneer and cream and cook over low heat for 5 minutes. Do not boil hard.
Crush dried fenugreek between the palms, stir it in and serve.

title: Malai Kofta
description: A practical home-style Malai Kofta recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine the mince, grated onion, gram flour, half the ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, coriander, half the garam masala and salt. Knead by hand for 3 to 4 minutes until slightly sticky.
With lightly oiled hands, shape into 16 equal balls. Chill for 20 minutes so they hold their shape.
Heat oil in a wide pot over medium heat. Fry the sliced onions for 8 to 10 minutes until golden, then add the remaining ginger-garlic paste for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes, yogurt and ground spices. Cook over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until the masala thickens and the oil separates.
Add water and bring to a gentle simmer. Lower the koftas into the gravy in one layer; do not stir for the first 8 minutes.
Cover and simmer gently for 18 to 22 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until the koftas reach at least 71°C (160°F) internally.
Uncover and reduce the gravy for 5 minutes if needed. Finish with garam masala and coriander and rest for 5 minutes before serving.

title: Sarson Saag
description: A practical home-style Sarson Saag recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 750 g spinach, mustard greens or mixed leafy greens
steps: Wash and dry the vegetables thoroughly, then cut them into even pieces so they cook at the same rate.
Heat oil in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add whole spices or aromatics and stir for 30 to 45 seconds until fragrant.
Add firm vegetables first and sauté for 4 to 6 minutes. Add quick-cooking leafy vegetables later so they retain colour and texture.
Add tomatoes, sauce ingredients and ground spices. Cook over medium heat for 5 to 8 minutes, stirring every minute.
Add only the measured water or stock, cover and cook until just tender. Avoid overcooking; most vegetables need 8 to 15 minutes.
Uncover and cook over high heat for 2 to 4 minutes to remove excess moisture and concentrate the sauce.
Finish with lemon, herbs, sesame oil, cream or garam masala as listed and serve immediately.

title: Amritsari Fish
description: A practical home-style Amritsari Fish recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Pat the fish dry and cut into even pieces. Mix ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, chilli, turmeric, coriander, carom seeds and salt and coat the fish for 20 minutes.
Whisk gram flour, rice flour and water for 1 to 2 minutes into a smooth, thick batter that coats the back of a spoon.
Heat 5 to 7 cm oil in a deep pan to 175°C (350°F).
Dip each fish piece in batter, let the excess drip off and fry in small batches for 4 to 6 minutes, turning once.
The coating should be crisp and golden and the centre should reach 63°C (145°F).
Drain on a rack, sprinkle with chaat masala and serve immediately with lemon.

title: Paneer Tikka
description: A practical home-style Paneer Tikka recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, oil, salt and spices for 1 minute until smooth.
Add the paneer and turn until every piece is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours; tougher beef or mutton cuts may marinate for 6 to 8 hours.
Preheat a grill, barbecue or oven broiler to high, about 220°C (425°F). If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Thread the pieces on skewers or arrange them on an oiled rack with space between each piece. Brush lightly with oil.
Cook for 12 to 20 minutes depending on size, turning every 4 to 5 minutes and basting once, until browned at the edges.
Check the thickest piece for doneness; it should reach 71°C (160°F) for minced meat.
Rest for 5 minutes, then serve with lemon, sliced onion and chutney.

title: Tandoori Paneer
description: A practical home-style Tandoori Paneer recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.

Add the paneer and turn until every piece is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours; tougher beef or mutton cuts may marinate for 6 to 8 hours.
Preheat a grill, barbecue or oven broiler to high, about 220°C (425°F). If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes.

title: Pav Bhaji
description: A practical home-style Pav Bhaji recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Boil potatoes, cauliflower and peas until very tender, then drain and mash coarsely.
Heat butter on a wide tawa or skillet over medium heat. Cook onion and bell pepper for 6 to 8 minutes until soft.
Add tomatoes, pav bhaji masala, chilli and salt and cook for 8 minutes until thick.
Add the mashed vegetables and 1 cup water. Mash and stir continuously for 10 to 12 minutes until smooth and glossy.
Split the pav, spread with butter and toast cut-side down for 1 to 2 minutes.
Serve the bhaji hot with pav, lemon, onion and coriander.

title: Vada Pav
description: A practical home-style Vada Pav recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Prepare the filling completely before assembling. Cook meat over medium-high heat until browned and safely done, or fry the patties until crisp and hot in the centre.
Warm the bread, bun or paratha on a dry griddle over medium heat for 30 to 60 seconds per side.
Spread the listed chutney, sauce or dressing evenly, leaving a 1 cm border around the edge.
Arrange the hot filling in an even layer, followed by onion, tomato, pickles, cheese or herbs as listed.
For rolls, fold the lower edge over the filling and roll tightly. For pressed sandwiches, cook at medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes per side until the cheese melts.
Serve immediately while the bread is warm and the filling remains hot.

title: Misal Pav
description: A practical home-style Misal Pav recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Cook the sprouted moth beans with turmeric, salt and water until tender, about 15 minutes.
Heat oil and cook onion, ginger-garlic, tomato and misal masala for 8 to 10 minutes until the oil separates.
Add the cooked sprouts and enough water for a moderately thin curry. Simmer for 12 minutes.
Prepare the spicy tarri separately if listed and spoon it over the curry.
Serve in bowls topped with farsan, onion, coriander and lemon, with toasted pav on the side.

title: Puran Poli
description: A practical home-style Puran Poli recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix the flour, oil and water and knead for 6 to 8 minutes into a soft, smooth dough. Cover and rest for 30 minutes.
Cook the chana dal until tender but not watery, then drain thoroughly. Cook it with jaggery over medium-low heat for 8 to 10 minutes until the filling is dry.
Cool the filling, add cardamom and grind or mash until smooth. Divide the dough and filling into equal balls.
Flatten one dough ball, enclose one filling ball and seal. Roll gently to a 15 to 18 cm circle using minimal flour.
Cook on a hot griddle over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side, applying ghee after the first turn.
Serve warm; stack under a clean cloth to keep soft.

title: Bharli Vangi
description: A practical home-style Bharli Vangi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.

title: Sabudana Khichdi
description: A practical home-style Sabudana Khichdi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2 cups sabudana, rinsed and soaked 5 hours
steps: Rinse sabudana until the water is clear, drain completely and soak covered for 4 to 6 hours with only enough water to reach the top.
Check that the pearls crush easily between the fingers, then mix with ground peanuts, salt and sugar.
Heat ghee over medium heat and fry cumin and green chilli for 30 seconds.
Add potato and cook for 5 to 7 minutes until lightly golden.
Add sabudana and cook for 4 to 6 minutes, tossing gently, until the pearls turn translucent. Do not overcook or they become sticky.
Add lemon and coriander and serve immediately.

title: Thalipeeth
description: A practical home-style Thalipeeth recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix the flour, onion, chilli, sesame, cumin, turmeric, coriander and salt.
Add water gradually and knead for 2 to 3 minutes into a soft, non-sticky dough. Rest for 10 minutes.
Place a dough ball on oiled parchment or a damp cloth and pat into a 15 cm circle about 4 mm thick. Make a small hole in the centre.
Transfer to a hot griddle over medium heat, drizzle oil around the edges and into the centre hole.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes per side until browned and cooked through.
Serve hot with yogurt or butter.

title: Zunka Bhakar
description: A practical home-style Zunka Bhakar recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 4 jowar or bajra bhakri for serving
steps: Heat oil over medium heat and crackle mustard and cumin for 30 seconds.
Add onion and green chilli and cook for 5 to 6 minutes until soft.
Whisk gram flour with water, turmeric and salt until smooth, then pour it into the pan while stirring continuously.
Cook over low heat for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring and breaking up lumps, until thick and crumbly.
Cover for 2 minutes, then fluff and serve hot with bhakar.

title: Batata Vada
description: A practical home-style Batata Vada recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon oil and fry mustard seeds and curry leaves for 30 seconds. Add ginger-chilli paste and turmeric.
Mix in mashed potatoes, salt and lemon and cook for 2 minutes. Cool and shape into 10 balls.
Whisk gram flour, rice flour, chilli, salt and water for 1 minute into a smooth, thick batter.
Heat oil to 175°C (350°F). Dip each potato ball in batter and fry for 4 to 5 minutes until crisp and golden.
Drain on a rack and serve hot with chutney.

title: Kolhapuri Chicken
description: A practical home-style Kolhapuri Chicken recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 tsp paprika or red chilli powder
steps: Dry-roast the listed whole spices and coconut over medium-low heat until fragrant, cool and grind to a fine masala.
Heat oil and brown the chicken over medium-high heat for 6 to 8 minutes; remove.
In the same pan, cook onion for 8 minutes, then add ginger-garlic paste for 1 minute.
Add tomato and the ground Kolhapuri masala and cook for 6 to 8 minutes until the oil separates.
Return the chicken, add water, cover and simmer for 18 to 22 minutes until it reaches 74°C (165°F).
Uncover and reduce for 5 minutes, adjust salt and garnish with coriander.

title: Mysore Pak
description: A practical home-style Mysore Pak recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Measure all ingredients before starting and warm the milk, water or syrup separately.
Heat the ghee or butter in a heavy pan over medium-low heat. Add the flour, semolina or other base and roast for 10 to 20 minutes, stirring continuously, until evenly golden and nutty.
Add the warm liquid gradually in 3 to 4 additions while whisking continuously to prevent lumps and splashing.
Add sugar and cook over low heat, stirring and folding from the bottom every 20 to 30 seconds.
Continue until the mixture becomes glossy, leaves the sides of the pan and holds a soft mound, usually 8 to 15 minutes.
Mix in cardamom and nuts. Serve warm, or press into a greased tray, cool completely and cut into pieces.

title: Kaju Katli
description: A practical home-style Kaju Katli recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Grind the cashews in short pulses to a fine powder without releasing oil.
Cook sugar and water over medium heat for 4 to 5 minutes until a one-string syrup forms.
Lower the heat, add cashew powder and stir continuously for 6 to 8 minutes until the mixture leaves the pan.
Transfer to parchment and cool for 3 minutes. Knead gently for 1 minute while still warm.
Roll to 5 mm thickness, cool completely and cut into diamonds.

title: Sandesh
description: A practical home-style Sandesh recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Curdle hot milk with lemon juice, strain and rinse the chenna under cold water.
Hang for 30 minutes, then knead the chenna for 6 to 8 minutes until very smooth.
Cook with powdered sugar over low heat for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring continuously; do not dry it completely.
Remove when the mixture leaves the pan but remains soft. Add cardamom.
Cool until warm, shape into discs and garnish with pistachios.

title: Peda
description: A practical home-style Peda recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat ghee in a non-stick pan over low heat. Add condensed milk and milk powder and mix for 1 minute until smooth.
Cook over low heat for 8 to 12 minutes, stirring continuously, until the mixture leaves the sides.
Add cardamom and cool until safe to handle.
Grease the hands lightly, knead for 1 minute and divide into equal balls.
Flatten, garnish with pistachios and cool completely.

title: Motichoor Ladoo
description: A practical home-style Motichoor Ladoo recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk gram flour with water for 1 to 2 minutes into a smooth, flowing batter.
Heat oil or ghee to 175°C (350°F). Pour batter through the appropriate perforated spoon and fry the tiny boondi for 30 to 45 seconds; do not brown.
Prepare a one-string sugar syrup and add cardamom.
Mix the warm boondi with syrup and seeds or nuts and rest for 10 minutes.
While still warm, press firmly into equal balls. Cool completely before storing.

title: Besan Ladoo
description: A practical home-style Besan Ladoo recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat ghee over medium-low heat. Add gram flour and roast for 18 to 25 minutes, stirring continuously, until deep golden and nutty.
Remove from heat and cool for 15 minutes; the mixture should be warm, not hot.
Mix in powdered sugar and cardamom for 2 minutes until evenly combined.
Fold in nuts and press into equal balls.
Cool completely before storing airtight.

title: Boondi Ladoo
description: A practical home-style Boondi Ladoo recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk gram flour with water for 1 to 2 minutes into a smooth, flowing batter.
Heat oil or ghee to 175°C (350°F). Pour batter through the appropriate perforated spoon and fry the tiny boondi for 30 to 45 seconds; do not brown.
Prepare a one-string sugar syrup and add cardamom.
Mix the warm boondi with syrup and seeds or nuts and rest for 10 minutes.
While still warm, press firmly into equal balls. Cool completely before storing.

title: Malpua
description: A practical home-style Malpua recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Prepare the syrup first. Boil sugar and water for 5 to 7 minutes, then add cardamom, lemon or rose water and keep warm at about 70°C (160°F).
Mix or knead the dough or batter only until smooth. For milk-solid doughs, knead gently for 2 to 3 minutes; for fermented batters, rest as directed until bubbly.
Shape equal portions or transfer batter to a piping bag. Keep pieces small enough to cook through evenly.
Heat oil or ghee to 150 to 160°C (300 to 320°F) for milk sweets, or 175°C (350°F) for flour-based fritters.
Fry in small batches, turning frequently, until evenly coloured and cooked through. Adjust the heat so the exterior does not darken before the centre cooks.
Drain for 20 seconds, then place directly into warm syrup. Soak for 20 minutes to 2 hours depending on the sweet.
Serve warm or at room temperature, and refrigerate milk-based sweets within 2 hours.

title: Shrikhand
description: A practical home-style Shrikhand recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Pinch of saffron soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk
steps: Line a sieve with muslin, add yogurt and drain in the refrigerator for 8 hours until very thick.
Transfer the hung yogurt to a bowl and whisk by hand for 2 minutes until smooth.
Add powdered sugar gradually and whisk for another 2 minutes until glossy.
Fold in cardamom and saffron milk with a spatula.
Chill for at least 2 hours and garnish with nuts before serving.

title: Basundi
description: A practical home-style Basundi recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse and soak the rice when listed. Heat the milk in a heavy, wide pan over medium heat until steaming, stirring every 2 minutes.
Reduce to low heat, add rice or ground rice and cardamom, and simmer gently for 35 to 50 minutes. Scrape the base and sides every 3 to 4 minutes to prevent scorching.
When the grains are completely soft and the milk has reduced, add sugar and stir until dissolved.
Continue cooking for 8 to 12 minutes until the dessert coats the back of a spoon. Add cornstarch slurry only when listed and simmer for 2 minutes.
Stir in rose water, saffron or nuts after turning off the heat.
Serve warm, or cool to room temperature within 1 hour and refrigerate for at least 3 hours before serving chilled.

title: Payasam
description: A practical home-style Payasam recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse the cleaned trotters or head-and-foot pieces thoroughly. Blanch in boiling water for 8 minutes, drain and rinse again to remove impurities.
Heat oil or ghee over medium heat and fry the onion for 8 to 10 minutes until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add yogurt and spices gradually, stirring for 4 to 5 minutes until smooth and glossy.

Add the blanched pieces and water. Bring to a boil, skim the surface, then cover and simmer on the lowest heat for 4 to 6 hours, or pressure-cook for 60 to 75 minutes, until the gelatin releases and the meat is tender.
Remove any loose bones, then simmer uncovered for 15 to 20 minutes to reach a lightly thick, silky consistency.
Adjust salt and garam masala. Keep the soup above 74°C (165°F) when serving.
Garnish with ginger, coriander, green chilli and lemon, and serve with naan.

title: Modak
description: A practical home-style Modak recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Cook coconut and jaggery over medium-low heat for 6 to 8 minutes until the mixture is moist but no longer watery; add cardamom and cool.
Bring water, ghee or oil and salt to a boil. Lower the heat, add rice flour and stir vigorously for 1 minute.
Cover and rest for 5 minutes, then knead the warm dough for 3 to 4 minutes until smooth.
Divide into equal portions, flatten each into a cup, add filling and seal the top.
Steam over boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes until the surface looks glossy.
Rest for 2 minutes and serve warm.

title: Kozhukattai
description: A practical home-style Kozhukattai recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.

Steam over boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes until the surface looks glossy.

title: Ariselu
description: A practical home-style Ariselu recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Make a thick jaggery syrup that reaches soft-ball stage, about 115 to 118°C (239 to 244°F).
Stir in cardamom and sesame, then add rice flour gradually until a soft dough forms.
Rest covered for 20 minutes. Divide and pat into small discs with greased fingers.
Heat ghee or oil to 165°C (330°F) and fry for 2 to 3 minutes per side until deep golden.
Press gently between two flat spoons to remove excess oil and cool.

title: Pootharekulu
description: A practical home-style Pootharekulu recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Prepare a very thin, lump-free rice-starch batter and keep it covered so it does not dry.
Heat an inverted smooth pot or special hot plate over medium heat until a drop of water evaporates quickly.
Dip a clean cloth in batter and swipe once over the hot surface to create a paper-thin sheet; lift it immediately when dry.
Brush each sheet lightly with ghee and sprinkle with powdered sugar, cardamom and nuts.
Layer several sheets, fold into a neat parcel and store airtight.

title: Dharwad Peda
description: A practical home-style Dharwad Peda recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat ghee and khoya over medium-low heat and cook for 12 to 15 minutes, stirring continuously, until deep golden-brown.
Add sugar and milk and continue stirring for 5 to 7 minutes until the mixture thickens and leaves the pan.
Add cardamom and cool until warm.
Shape into small discs and roll lightly in fine sugar if desired.
Cool completely before serving.

title: Chhena Poda
description: A practical home-style Chhena Poda recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (356°F) and grease the specified pan. For steamed versions, bring water to a steady boil in the steamer instead.
Measure the dry ingredients accurately and whisk them together for 30 seconds so the leavening is evenly distributed.
In a separate bowl, whisk the wet ingredients for 1 to 2 minutes until smooth. Add the dry mixture and fold only until no dry patches remain.
Add fruit, nuts, vegetables or cheese as listed. Add baking soda or fruit salt last and fold gently for 15 to 20 seconds.
Transfer immediately to the pan. Bake until set and golden, generally 25 to 40 minutes, or steam for 15 to 20 minutes as appropriate.
A skewer should come out clean from a baked item. Cool for 10 minutes before cutting, glazing or adding syrup.

title: Khaja
description: A practical home-style Khaja recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix flour, ghee and water and knead for 5 minutes into a firm dough. Rest for 30 minutes.
Roll thin sheets, spread with the cornstarch-ghee paste, stack and roll tightly into a log.
Slice and roll each piece lightly without crushing the layers.
Heat oil to 160°C (320°F) and fry slowly for 6 to 8 minutes until crisp and golden.
Dip in warm one-string syrup for 30 seconds, drain and cool.

title: Obbattu
description: A practical home-style Obbattu recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Knead flour, oil and water for 6 to 8 minutes into a soft dough. Cover and rest for 30 minutes.
Cook chana dal until tender, drain thoroughly and cook with jaggery over medium-low heat until dry.
Cool, add cardamom and grind to a smooth filling. Divide dough and filling into equal balls.
Enclose filling inside dough, seal and roll gently to a thin circle.
Cook on a medium-hot griddle for 1 to 2 minutes per side with ghee.

title: Mohanthal
description: A practical home-style Mohanthal recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Measure all ingredients before starting and warm the milk, water or syrup separately.
Heat the ghee or butter in a heavy pan over medium-low heat. Add the flour, semolina or other base and roast for 10 to 20 minutes, stirring continuously, until evenly golden and nutty.
Add the warm liquid gradually in 3 to 4 additions while whisking continuously to prevent lumps and splashing.
Add sugar and cook over low heat, stirring and folding from the bottom every 20 to 30 seconds.
Continue until the mixture becomes glossy, leaves the sides of the pan and holds a soft mound, usually 8 to 15 minutes.
Mix in cardamom and nuts. Serve warm, or press into a greased tray, cool completely and cut into pieces.

title: Amritsari Kulcha
description: A practical home-style Amritsari Kulcha recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine flour, yeast or leavening, sugar and salt. Add the wet ingredients and mix with a spoon until a rough dough forms.
Knead by hand for 8 to 10 minutes, or with a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook for 6 minutes on medium-low speed, until smooth and elastic.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for 60 to 90 minutes, until doubled. Unleavened doughs need only a 20 to 30 minute rest.
Divide evenly, shape and add any filling or topping. Rest the shaped dough for 10 to 20 minutes while heating the cooking surface.
For oven-baked naan or enriched breads, preheat to 230°C (445°F) and bake for 6 to 12 minutes until puffed and browned. For griddle breads, cook over medium-high heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Brush with butter or ghee immediately after cooking and keep covered with a clean cloth until serving.

title: Bhatura
description: A practical home-style Bhatura recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine flour, yeast or leavening, sugar and salt. Add the wet ingredients and mix with a spoon until a rough dough forms.
Knead by hand for 8 to 10 minutes, or with a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook for 6 minutes on medium-low speed, until smooth and elastic.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for 60 to 90 minutes, until doubled. Unleavened doughs need only a 20 to 30 minute rest.
Divide evenly, shape and add any filling or topping. Rest the shaped dough for 10 to 20 minutes while heating the cooking surface.
For oven-baked naan or enriched breads, preheat to 230°C (445°F) and bake for 6 to 12 minutes until puffed and browned. For griddle breads, cook over medium-high heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Brush with butter or ghee immediately after cooking and keep covered with a clean cloth until serving.

title: Mathri
description: A practical home-style Mathri recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine the dry ingredients and spices in a bowl. Rub in the measured fat for 2 to 3 minutes until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add water gradually and knead for 3 to 5 minutes to form the required firm dough. Cover and rest for 20 minutes.
Shape, roll, press or slice the dough evenly so all pieces cook at the same rate. Keep unshaped dough covered.
Heat oil in a deep pan to 165 to 175°C (330 to 350°F), using the lower end for thicker snacks.
Fry in small batches, turning as needed, until crisp and evenly golden, usually 4 to 7 minutes. Maintain the oil temperature between batches.
Drain on a rack and cool completely before storing in an airtight container.

title: Masala Chai
description: A practical home-style Masala Chai recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2 tsp tea leaves or green tea
steps: Bring the measured water to a steady boil. Add tea, coffee or whole spices and simmer for the specified infusion time, usually 2 to 5 minutes.
Add milk when listed and lower the heat to prevent boiling over. Simmer gently for another 3 to 5 minutes.
Add sugar and stir for 30 seconds until dissolved. For foamy tea, whisk for 20 to 30 seconds or pour carefully between two heatproof jugs.
Strain into warmed cups and serve immediately. Keep milk-based drinks above 60°C (140°F) if holding briefly.
Serve immediately in warmed cups; refrigerate any milk-based remainder within 2 hours.

title: Thandai
description: A practical home-style Thandai recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Chill the liquid ingredients and serving glasses before starting.
Add the main liquid, fruit, yogurt, nuts, herbs, sugar and seasonings to a blender in that order.
Blend on low speed for 20 seconds, then on high speed for 45 to 60 seconds until completely smooth.
Taste and adjust sweetness, salt or acidity. Add chilled water or ice gradually to reach the desired consistency.
Strain only when a clear drink is desired, then pour into cold glasses and serve immediately.

title: Bhel Puri
description: A practical home-style Bhel Puri recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Prepare all cooked components first and cool them to room temperature. Keep crisp items in an airtight container until the final minute.
Whisk the yogurt for 1 minute until smooth, adding 1 to 2 tablespoons water if needed for a pourable consistency.
Arrange the potatoes, chickpeas, patties, puri, puffed rice or momos specified in the recipe in a wide bowl or individual plates.
Spoon over the tamarind chutney and green chutney evenly, followed by yogurt when listed.
Sprinkle with chaat masala, roasted cumin, chilli, onion and tomato. Toss gently for 20 to 30 seconds only when the dish is meant to be mixed.
Finish with sev, coriander, peanuts or pomegranate and serve immediately while the textures are crisp and fresh.

title: Pani Puri
description: A practical home-style Pani Puri recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Blend mint, coriander, green chilli, ginger, lemon, cumin, chaat masala and black salt with 1 cup water for 60 seconds until smooth.
Strain into a jug, add the remaining chilled water and whisk for 30 seconds. Taste and balance with more lemon, salt or chilli, then chill for at least 30 minutes.
Mix the boiled potato and chickpeas with a little chaat masala and salt. Keep the filling, chutney and flavoured water in separate bowls.
Just before eating, tap a small opening in each puri and add 1 to 2 teaspoons filling.
Add 1/2 teaspoon tamarind chutney, fill with the chilled spicy water and serve immediately.
Do not assemble in advance, because the shells soften within minutes.

title: Kathi Roll
description: A practical home-style Kathi Roll recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Prepare the filling completely before assembling. Cook meat over medium-high heat until browned and safely done, or fry the patties until crisp and hot in the centre.
Warm the bread, bun or paratha on a dry griddle over medium heat for 30 to 60 seconds per side.
Spread the listed chutney, sauce or dressing evenly, leaving a 1 cm border around the edge.
Arrange the hot filling in an even layer, followed by onion, tomato, pickles, cheese or herbs as listed.
For rolls, fold the lower edge over the filling and roll tightly. For pressed sandwiches, cook at medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes per side until the cheese melts.
Serve immediately while the bread is warm and the filling remains hot.

title: Tawa Pulao
description: A practical home-style Tawa Pulao recipe from Indian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Rinse the rice 3 to 4 times, soak for 20 to 30 minutes and drain.
Heat oil or ghee in a heavy pot over medium heat. Fry the onion for 8 to 10 minutes until evenly golden, then add the whole spices and stir for 30 seconds.
Add the listed meat or vegetables and sauté for 4 to 6 minutes. For meat pulao, add stock and simmer until the meat is nearly tender before adding rice.
Add the drained rice and the measured hot liquid. Use approximately 1 3/4 cups liquid per cup of soaked basmati rice, adjusting for any liquid already in the pot.
Season with salt, bring to a brisk boil and cook uncovered for 5 to 7 minutes, until the liquid level drops just below the rice.
Cover tightly, reduce to the lowest heat and steam for 15 minutes. Do not stir while the rice is steaming.
Turn off the heat, rest covered for 10 minutes, then fluff from the sides with a fork and serve.

title: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A practical home-style Spaghetti Carbonara recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 150 g guanciale or beef bacon, diced
steps: Bring 4 litres water to a rolling boil and add 1 tablespoon salt. Cook the spaghetti until just al dente, reserving 1 1/2 cups pasta water.
While the pasta cooks, render the diced guanciale or beef bacon in a wide skillet over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until crisp. Turn off the heat.
Whisk the eggs, yolk, Pecorino and black pepper for 1 minute into a thick paste. Whisk in 2 tablespoons warm pasta water.
Add the drained pasta to the skillet and toss for 30 seconds in the rendered fat.
Remove the skillet completely from the heat. Pour in the egg mixture and toss vigorously for 60 to 90 seconds, adding pasta water 1 tablespoon at a time until glossy and creamy.
Do not return to direct heat or the eggs may scramble. Serve immediately with extra cheese and pepper.

title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A practical home-style Spaghetti Aglio e Olio recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Bring 4 litres water to a rolling boil and add 1 tablespoon salt. Cook the pasta until 1 minute short of al dente, reserving 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Heat the oil, butter or rendered fat in a wide pan over medium heat. Cook the aromatics for 30 seconds to 5 minutes according to the recipe, without burning the garlic.
Add tomatoes, vegetables, seafood or meat and cook until the sauce ingredients are properly cooked and the sauce is concentrated.
Transfer the drained pasta directly into the sauce. Toss over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes, adding pasta water 2 tablespoons at a time.
Add cheese, herbs, cream or finishing oil off the heat when appropriate. Toss for another 30 seconds until the sauce clings to every piece.
Taste for salt and acidity and serve immediately on warm plates.

title: Tortellini in Brodo
description: A practical home-style Tortellini in Brodo recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 500 g fresh cheese or meat tortellini
2 litres clear chicken or beef broth
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Place broth, onion, carrot, celery and bay leaf in a pot and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce to low heat and simmer for 20 minutes, then strain the broth and return it to the pot.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Bring the clear broth back to a steady simmer.
Add fresh tortellini and cook according to their size, usually 3 to 5 minutes, until tender but not split.
Ladle immediately into warm bowls with enough broth to cover and serve with Parmesan.

title: Ravioli Ricotta e Spinaci
description: A practical home-style Ravioli Ricotta e Spinaci recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 250 g spinach, cooked, squeezed dry and chopped
steps: Mix ricotta, spinach, egg, Parmesan, nutmeg, salt and pepper for 1 minute until evenly combined.
Place teaspoon portions of filling on half the pasta sheets, leaving at least 4 cm between portions.
Brush around the filling with water, cover with another sheet and press out all trapped air before cutting and sealing the ravioli.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a gentle boil and cook the ravioli for 3 to 5 minutes until they float and the pasta is tender.
Melt butter with sage over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes until fragrant and lightly golden.
Lift the ravioli into the butter, toss gently for 30 seconds and serve with Parmesan.

title: Risotto alla Milanese
description: A practical home-style Risotto alla Milanese recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Keep the stock at a gentle simmer in a separate saucepan throughout cooking.
Heat butter and olive oil in a wide pan over medium heat. Cook the onion for 4 to 5 minutes until translucent without browning.
Add the rice and toast for 2 minutes, stirring continuously, until the edges look translucent. Add wine when listed and cook until nearly evaporated.
Add hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring every 20 to 30 seconds and waiting until each addition is mostly absorbed before adding more.
Continue for 16 to 20 minutes until the rice is al dente and flows slowly when the pan is tilted. Add seafood or vegetables at the stage needed for their cooking time.
Remove from heat. Beat in Parmesan and cold butter vigorously for 1 minute to emulsify the risotto.
Rest for 2 minutes and serve immediately; risotto should spread softly rather than stand in a mound.

description: A practical home-style Risotto ai Funghi recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
